description: Defines repository responsibilities, persistence boundaries, and forbidden repository dependencies.

rules.name: Repositories own SQL
rules.description: SQL statements and direct database operations must live in repositories or dedicated persistence infrastructure.

rules.name: Repositories do not know HTTP
rules.description: Repositories must not import route utilities, handlers, frontend field names, or HTTP request/response concepts.

rules.name: Repositories return domain-level data
rules.description: Repositories should return domain objects, DTOs, booleans, or repository-level results, not transport response dictionaries.

rules.name: Repositories map rows centrally
rules.description: Database row to domain object conversion should be centralized through mapper functions or repository-owned mapping helpers.

rules.name: Repositories accept typed inputs
rules.description: Repository methods should accept DTOs or domain objects rather than primitive parameter lists when a use case has structured input.

rules.name: Repositories should avoid business orchestration
rules.description: Repositories should focus on persistence operations and data retrieval/update logic and should not coordinate multi-step business workflows or application orchestration.
